raw_consumer_statement:
A number of our credit cards have been compromised of the last 8 years. I am contacting CFPB to suggest that ALL credit cards have a second validation such as entering a 4 digit code when shopping in person or to a code automatically sent to the owners phone and the code is entered as validation.

A similar 4 digit code is required on Debit cards so why can't credit card use the same validation? It is terrible when our credit cards are compromised. If we use the credit card for a number of auto-pays we must contact ALL the companies that we are using a different card now. Also someone has to absorb the charges which is usually the merchant who accepted the card. Showing your ID isn't mandatory either plus you can not show an ID for on-line purchases. 

Many on-line stores who offer credit cards will text a code to verify that you are indeed the owner of the card. The same thing could be implemented for in-store use. it only takes a few seconds to get a code and enter it. 

I hope that some type of validation can be added to ALL credit card purchases. All the credit card companies I suggested this to stated it is against the law for credit cards to require this. Then why can Debit cards mandate a code?

Please help curb these fraudulent charges that happen hundreds if not thousands times a day. 

I thank you for consider these solutions to a very serious problem.
